question_text: Why are X-rays used in medical imaging rather than visible light?

options.label: A
options.text: X-rays have a longer wavelength so they can penetrate skin more easily

options.label: B
options.text: X-rays have a shorter wavelength and can penetrate soft tissues and pass through body parts at different rates, allowing internal structures to be seen

options.label: C
options.text: X-rays are cheaper to produce than visible light

options.label: D
options.text: X-rays do not harm the body at all

explanation: X-rays have a much shorter wavelength than visible light, which allows them to penetrate through skin and soft tissues. Different body tissues absorb different amounts of X-rays, so bones (which absorb more) appear white while soft tissues appear grey. This creates an image of internal structures.
